javadoc_before:
/** * Returns a String version of a shape to be used for the stored value. This method in Solr is only called if for some * reason a Shape object is passed to the field type (perhaps via a custom UpdateRequestProcessor), * *and* the field is marked as stored. The default implementation throws an exception. *

* Spatial4j 0.4 is probably the last release to support SpatialContext.toString(shape) but it's deprecated with no * planned replacement. Shapes do have a toString() method but they are generally internal/diagnostic and not * standard WKT. * The solution is subclassing and calling ctx.toString(shape) or directly using LegacyShapeReadWriterFormat or * passing in some sort of custom wrapped shape that holds a reference to a String or can generate it. */
javadoc_after: /** * Returns a String version of a shape to be used for the stored value. * * The format can be selected using the initParam format={WKT|GeoJSON} */
